methodology: 3 runs per query, median reported. Brain API on localhost (127.0.0.1:8000). Bare LLM via OpenRouter (meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free). Timing includes full round-trip from request to response.

cache_hits.note: facts_cache method — pre-verified answers returned from structured knowledge store

queries.query: What does HTTP status code 418 mean?
What is the capital of Australia?
What is the boiling point of water in Celsius?
What is the speed of light in meters per second?

llm_fallback.note: llm method — cache miss, falls through to LLM with verification layer overhead

queries.query: What is the maximum daily dose of acetaminophen for adults?
What is the 2024 401k contribution limit for employees under 50?
What is the Social Security full retirement age for people born after 1960?
What is the FDIC insurance limit per depositor?
What is the federal minimum wage?
How many justices are on the US Supreme Court?

bare_llm.note: Raw LLM call, no verification. Fast because no cache lookup, no cross-check, no structured knowledge query.

queries.query: What is the maximum daily dose of acetaminophen for adults?
What is the 2024 401k contribution limit for employees under 50?
What is the Social Security full retirement age for people born after 1960?
What is the FDIC insurance limit per depositor?
What is the federal minimum wage?
What does HTTP status code 418 mean?
What is the capital of Australia?
What is the boiling point of water in Celsius?
How many justices are on the US Supreme Court?
What is the speed of light in meters per second?

summary: Cache hits averaged 944ms vs 55ms for bare LLM — bare LLM is ~17x faster on raw latency. This is expected: Brain API spends time doing what the bare LLM skips entirely — structured knowledge lookup and claim verification. The tradeoff is accuracy: Llama scored 68% on hallucination and 43% on freshness. Brain API scored 100% on hallucination. Cache hits (944ms) are 2.5x faster than LLM fallback (2382ms) within Brain API itself.
